title: Savory Molten Saudi Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A nutty and molten Saudi Arabian dish featuring minced garlic, cooked in a bain-marie and following ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly principles, this tagine is a show-off dish for any occasion.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the lamb, garlic, onion, ginger, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lamb mixture to the pot and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chicken broth and dates to the pot, stirring to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
